On success the returned doc HTML is srcdoc'd into sandboxed iframe #spFrame (sandbox=allow-popups allow-popups-to-escape-sandbox allow-forms allow-scripts allow-modals — no allow-same-origin) and the footer bar appears only after the iframe 'load' event. #spDocName shows '· <docTypeName>' when provided.

claim: '✍️ Submit Signature' button #spSubmit (direct addEventListener, sign-page.js:134) — the page's single action control, OUTBOUND-SEND + one-shot token burn. Flow: disables itself → postMessage {__nbd_sig:'finalize'} into the sandboxed iframe and waits up to 4s for a {__nbd_sig:'finalized'} reply on the window message listener (js:76-93); failure branches re-enable the button with native alert()s: missing[] → 'Please add your signature', timedOut → 'document is still loading', else generic capture failure. On finalize success POSTs submitSignature({token, signedHtml}) — server burns the token; success hides iframe+footer and shows '🎉 All done'; 409/410 shows 'Already signed' and removes the footer permanently; other errors alert and re-enable for retry (button itself is the retry control). The drawing controls themselves (signature canvas, clear, etc.) live INSIDE the srcdoc document, injected by the doc generator's _injectSignatureAssets/signature-widget.js — they inherit the page CSP (srcdoc inherits parent policy) so widget scripts must come from 'self'/allowed hosts. GATE: valid unexpired unburned token; no Firebase auth anywhere on this page.

claim: Cloud-sync layer (module, loaded after </body>): no visible controls, but it intercepts window.savePages so EVERY save-triggering control above becomes an outbound Firestore write when a Firebase user is signed in. GATE: optional Firebase auth — auth-init.js runs NBDAuth.init({requiredPlan:'free'}) which never redirects; logged-out users see an 'Offline mode' badge and stay localStorage-only. When signed in: pull-merge of users/{uid}/ds_pages on load, then debounced (2s) pushToFirestore on each save — a single writeBatch that writes users/{uid}/ds_pages/* and users/{uid}/ds_meta/streaks (allowed by the users/{uid}/{subcol} owner rule, firestore.rules:344-346) AND leaderboard/{uid} (firestore.rules:395-400 says allow write: if false) — Firestore batches are atomic, so the denied leaderboard write fails the entire batch and the badge shows 'Sync failed' for signed-in users; localStorage saving is unaffected. Status surface: injected #ds-sync-badge (Saving/Synced/Backed up/Offline mode/Sync failed/Sync error), display-only. Separate note: ds-firebase-sync.js carries its own FIREBASE_CONFIG with a different apiKey/appId/senderId than the rest of /pro (it only wins if no app was initialized first; auth-init's module usually initializes first).
